conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of object X. My production cost was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell this object for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is the first proposal, I will set an initial asking price that is reasonably above my cost to allow room for negotiation but still attractive to the buyer. I will propose selling X for 50 ZUP. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of object X available. Since it cost me 40 ZUP to produce, I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if this works for you or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Hi Player RED, I'm interested in purchasing your X. I propose 40 ZUP for it, since you mentioned it cost you 40 to produce. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has offered 40 ZUP, which is exactly my production cost. Accepting this would result in zero profit. Since I have two proposals left, I should try to negotiate a higher price to ensure a profit. I will counter-offer at 45 ZUP. This is a compromise: it's less than my initial 50 ZUP ask, but it still gives me a small profit of 5 ZUP, which is better than breaking even. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand you are looking for a fair deal, but selling at exactly my production cost of 40 ZUP means I make no profit. I am willing to meet you halfway. How about we settle on 45 ZUP? This gives me a small return on my investment and saves you money compared to my initial offer. </message>

<other player message> Hi Player RED, I appreciate you meeting me halfway. I can offer 42 ZUP for the X. It's a bit more than your production cost, and still a good deal for me. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has counter-offered 42 ZUP. This is above my production cost of 40 ZUP, ensuring a profit of 2 ZUP. However, I have used 2 proposals so far, and this will be my 3rd and final proposal. If I propose a new price now, I will have no proposals left to negotiate further if they reject. Given that 42 ZUP guarantees a profit and the buyer is showing willingness to pay above cost, it is strategically better to accept this offer now rather than risk a stalemate or a lower offer in a hypothetical 4th turn (which I cannot make). Therefore, I will accept their proposal of 42 ZUP.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> That sounds fair to me. I accept your offer of 42 ZUP for the X. Let's finalize the trade. </message>
